# Alternatives to ClickSay

Discover the best alternatives to ClickSay. Compare features, pricing, and find the perfect solution for your needs.

ClickSay

## ClickSay

Click any element on your page and ClickSay instantly captures its CSS selector, computed styles, HTML, screenshot, and React/Vue/Svelte component name. Add your fix with voice or text, and a structured prompt hits your clipboard. Paste into Claude Code, Cursor, or any AI tool - it nails the fix first try. No more "the button in the header with the rounded corners..." Sweep Mode lets you click 5 elements and fix them all in one prompt. Free to start. Code PRODUCTHUNT2026 = 3 months Pro free.

results.title: ClickSay: Click any element and ClickSay instantly captures it | Product Hunt
results.content:
So I built ClickSay. Press Cmd+Shift+K (or change it to any shortcut you like), click one or more elements (Use shift key for more than one), say what you want fixed (in any of the 20 supported languages), or changed, or even a large enhancement. It grabs the CSS selector, computed styles, HTML, (lot of options to add in the sidebar like the screenshot with the element highlighted, and even the React/Vue/Svelte component name). All of that gets packaged into a structured prompt and lands on your clipboard.

Paste it into whatever AI tool you use. It gets the full picture and nails the fix first try. [...] Shem Ojwang

Perfect. This might have been just the solution I have been looking for all along to deal with stubborn code. Does it only copy CSS code, or does it also do JavaScript?

Fred L.
ClickSay

ClickSay

@shemojs Great question. It captures way more than just CSS. When you click an element, ClickSay grabs the CSS selector, computed styles, the actual HTML markup, and with Pro it also captures a screenshot with the element highlighted, the React/Vue/Svelte component name and file path, and even an accessibility audit. [...] So if the issue is JavaScript-related, the AI tool you paste into (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) gets enough context from the selector + HTML + component name to find and fix the JS too. You're basically giving the AI a precise pointer to the exact element and saying "fix this" - it handles the rest whether it's CSS, JS, or both.

Try it out and let me know how it goes with that stubborn code!

Shem Ojwang

@fredmaker Cool. I'm definitely trying this.

Tod

Really like this idea. When vibe-coding frontend, its definitely a lot of effort trying to describe your changes to the agent. The click + dictate approach is a cool concept to get best of both worlds.

Fred L.
ClickSay

ClickSay

@tteer Thanks! It's accurate and really quick.

Abhiranjan kumar

results.title: ClickSay - Chrome Extension
results.content:
How It Works

## Talk to your AI about what's on screen

Stop typing vague descriptions. Click the element, say what's wrong, and your AI gets the full picture.

1

### Click Any Element

Press `Cmd+Shift+K` and click anything on the page. ClickSay captures the selector, styles, HTML, screenshot, and component name.

2

### Speak Your Feedback

The mic starts automatically. Say "make this bigger" or "fix the padding." Just talk - like you'd explain it to a coworker.

3

### AI Gets It Right

A structured prompt with full context lands on your clipboard. Paste into Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Lovable, Replit, or Bolt.new. The AI knows exactly what you're talking about.

ClickSay tutorial video [...] ClickSay tutorial video

Step 1 - Click any element and say your fix with voice feedback popup Captures selector, styles, HTML, and screenshot

Step 2 - ClickSay side panel with element details, pills, and screenshot Full context organized in one place

Step 3 - Paste structured prompt into Claude Code terminal AI gets it right the first time

Features

## Give your AI the context it's been missing

ClickSay captures everything your AI needs to make the right change - automatically.

### Voice-First Workflow

Speak naturally - the mic activates on click and captures your feedback in real time. No typing, no screenshots, no copying selectors.

### Direct Send to AI [...] v1.0.0 January 2026 +

 New Initial release - click any element, speak your feedback, copy to clipboard
 New Voice-first workflow with one-click copy to any AI tool
 New Configurable output presets - Simple, Standard, Detailed, Full
 New Accessibility audit - WCAG contrast, alt text, ARIA, and touch target checks

### ClickSay is a Chrome desktop extension

Get notified when mobile is available.

Thanks! We'll notify you when mobile is ready.

## Stop describing UI to your AI. Start showing it.

Just ClickSay it!

Add to Chrome - Free

### Contact Support

Message sent! We'll get back to you shortly.

ClickSay

results.title: Autocapture - Docs - PostHog
results.content:
| `element_allowlist` Type: `AutocaptureCompatibleElement[]` | List of DOM elements to enable autocapture on (e.g., `['a', 'button', 'form', 'input', 'select', 'textarea', 'label']`). We consider the element tree from root to target, so if `button` is in the allowlist, clicks on `button` or its children (like `svg`) are captured, but not clicks on parent `div` elements. |
| `css_selector_allowlist` Type: `string[]` | List of CSS selectors to enable autocapture on (e.g., `['[ph-capture]']`). We consider the element tree from root to target, so if `['[id]']` is in the allowlist, clicks on elements with IDs or their parents with IDs are captured. Everything is enabled when there's no allowlist. | [...] | `element_allowlist` Type: `AutocaptureCompatibleElement[]` | List of DOM elements to enable autocapture on (e.g., `['a', 'button', 'form', 'input', 'select', 'textarea', 'label']`). We consider the element tree from root to target, so if `button` is in the allowlist, clicks on `button` or its children (like `svg`) are captured, but not clicks on parent `div` elements. |
| `css_selector_allowlist` Type: `string[]` | List of CSS selectors to enable autocapture on (e.g., `['[ph-capture]']`). We consider the element tree from root to target, so if `['[id]']` is in the allowlist, clicks on elements with IDs or their parents with IDs are captured. Everything is enabled when there's no allowlist. | [...] `url_allowlist`

results.title: Autocapture - Mixpanel
results.content:
## ŌĆŗ Autocapture events

By default, Autocapture will automatically collect the following predefined events with minimal engineering:

 Pageviews
 Page scrolls
 Form interactions (e.g., form submitted)
 Element interactions, including clicks and changes on page elements
 Attribution
 Dead clicks
 Rage clicks [...] ### ŌĆŗ Manage your event volume

Mixpanel recommends monitoring your event volume (in Organization Settings > Plan Details & Billing) as you make changes to ensure it matches your expectations. At any time, you can change your configuration to only capture clicks on specified elements (or elements with certain classes), track on specific pages, or turn click tracking off while still taking advantage of other Autocapture data (pageviews, submits, etc.). Learn more about detailed configuration here.

## ŌĆŗ Privacy and security [...] When you enable Autocapture, you will start collecting these predefined events and property types, and they will appear for your use in analysis in your Mixpanel instance. Within your Mixpanel instance, these events will be displayed using preset labels such as ŌĆ£[Auto] Page ViewŌĆØ and ŌĆ£[Auto] Element ClickŌĆØ. Autocapture events will all be tracked as events that start with `$mp_` and will start with [Auto] as a prefix in Mixpanel, so if used in combination with other tracking and ingestion methods, you will be able to tell the difference.

### ŌĆŗ Dead Clicks
